pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm bothering to wake up anymore. the house is just so quiet. it feels like i'm just walking around a museum of a life that doesn't exist anymore. everything is just... empty.

pool.baseline: honestly i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope and i can't stop thinking about how stupid everything is. like, everything we do, every single emotion, every bit of suffering... it's all just tiny sparks in a void that's eventually going to go cold and dark forever. it feels like we're just performing this long, painful play for an audience that doesn't exist and won't ever exist. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep going when the math just doesn't add up?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still doing this. everything i worked for in the lab just... evaporated. the funding is gone, my advisor won't even look me in the eye, and i feel like there's nothing left of me. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. it feels like i've been running a race for years only to realize the finish line doesn't exist. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to rest.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing but stubs of weeping wax, and the house is so quiet. It feels as though the very walls are mourning. After everything... after the betrayal... I feel like a ghost already. Like I am just a shadow waiting for the sun to never rise again. There is no path forward, only this heavy, hollow silence. Is there even any point in staying when the heart has already turned to ash?

pool.baseline: i was just sitting in this cafe in berlin and it hit me... like, if i just stopped showing up, if i just vanished tomorrow, nothing would actually change. the world would just keep spinning and nobody would even notice i was gone. it's like i'm just a ghost already.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left inside. i used to see colors and shapes in everything, like the world was talking to me through the canvas. now it’s just... grey. silent. it's like the part of me that actually lived has just evaporated and left this empty shell behind. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's just a vessel for breathing, but the soul is gone.
